findings.title: Default state filter silently hides closed PRs
findings.description: The default `stateFilter` changed from `''` (all states) to `'open'`. Existing users who bookmarked the page or relied on the default view will suddenly see fewer PRs. This is a UX-breaking behavior change — merged/closed PRs are hidden by default, and the empty-state message changes accordingly.

findings.title: setState called inside another setState updater
findings.description: The SSE `review-completed` handler calls `setReviews()` inside a `setFindings()` functional updater. React state updaters must be pure functions — dispatching a sibling setState as a side-effect violates React's contract and can produce stale closures or double-renders under concurrent mode. In Strict Mode, React may double-invoke updater functions, causing `setReviews` to be dispatched twice. The previous `findingsRef` pattern was correct for reading synchronous snapshot values; this refactor introduces a regression.

findings.title: FP count edge case when actionable=0
findings.description: When `actionableFindingCount` is explicitly `0` and `findingCount` is non-zero, `fpCount` = `findingCount`, implying all findings are false positives. This edge case is unlikely but the `actionableFindingCount` semantics may not match the assumption that `findingCount - actionableCount` always equals false positives.

findings.title: Reviews sorted twice in ReviewAccordion
findings.description: ReviewAccordion computes `reviewsOldestFirst` (ascending sort) and `reviewsNewestFirst` (descending sort) as two separate `useMemo` calls, each iterating and sorting the full reviews array. These could be derived from a single sort operation.

findings.title: ReviewCard ignores defaultExpanded after mount
findings.description: `ReviewCard` uses `useState(defaultExpanded)` — the prop only initializes state on mount. When a new review arrives via SSE and `reviewsNewestFirst` re-sorts, the previously-expanded card stays expanded while the new card gets `defaultExpanded=false`. The newest review should auto-expand but won't after the initial render.
